code: idvar <- unique({{source}}[["IDVAR"]]) idclass <- class({{domain}}[[idvar]]) idfunc <- get(paste0("as.", idclass))  supp_data <- {{source}} |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, IDVAR, IDVARVAL, QNAM, QVAL) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = QNAM, values_from = QVAL) |>   dplyr::mutate(IDVARVAL = idfunc(IDVARVAL)) |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, IDVARVAL, {{qnam}})  {{domain}} <- {{domain}} |>   dplyr::left_join(     y = supp_data,     by = dplyr::join_by(USUBJID, !!idvar == IDVARVAL)   )

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: Components let write data transformation reuse across studies swapping variable names render time. Instead copying modifying code new study, maintain single template. mighty component reusable code template single, well-defined data transformation step. Components commonly used generate ADaM (Analysis Dataset Model) programs, concept general: parameterized R code snippet reads data set, modifies , writes back can expressed component. Components combine two ideas: Mustache templating — placeholders like {{ domain }} filled render time, logic works across different data sets variables. Roxygen-like documentation — tags like @title, @param, @depends describe component , needs, produces. Think components reusable building blocks: one handles single derivation transformation, compose several build complete program.

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: minimal component doubles column. Every tag visible glance:
code: #' @title Double a variable #' @description #' Creates a new column that is twice the value of an existing column. #' #' @param domain `character` Name of the domain (data frame) #' @param input `character` Name of the existing column to double #' @param output `character` Name of the new column to create #' @type derivation #' @depends {{domain}} {{input}} #' @outputs {{output}} #' @code {{domain}} <- {{domain}} |>   dplyr::mutate(     {{output}} = 2 * {{input}}   )

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: Components use Mustache — simple, logic-less templating language. Inside @code, {{ }} Mustache placeholders, R syntax. text-replaced concrete values code parsed R. Rendering done whisker R package. three patterns used components : {{ variable }} — replaced value supplied render time. {{{ variable }}} — unescaped replacement. Used value literal R code (e.g., {{{ value }}} insert 1, "text", expression). {{#list}}...{{/list}} — repeats body element vector parameter. See Mustache manual full syntax reference.

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: input data set always called {{ domain }}. code must assign result back {{ domain }}. Use explicit package namespaces (dplyr::mutate(), mutate()). Joins must always specify explicit argument — enforced automatic validation (see Automatic code validation).

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: List built-standard components: Retrieve one name: Access individual fields active bindings: can also retrieve component local .mustache .R file passing file path get_component(). get_component() recommended entry point — handles built-standards custom files. know need built-standard, can also use get_standard() get_rendered_standard() directly.
code: list_standards() #> [1] "ady"         "aendt"       "assign"      "astdt"       "predecessor" #> [6] "supp_sdtm"   "trtemfl" ady <- get_component("ady") ady #> <mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: derivation #> Parameters: #> • domain: `character` Name of new domain being created #> • variable: `character` Name of new variable to create #> • date: `character` Name of date variable to use #> Depends: #> • {{domain}}.{{date}} #> • {{domain}}.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • {{variable}} ady$title #> [1] "Analysis relative day" ady$type #> [1] "derivation" ady$params #>       name                                  description #> 1   domain `character` Name of new domain being created #> 2 variable   `character` Name of new variable to create #> 3     date     `character` Name of date variable to use ady$depends #>       domain   column #> 1 {{domain}} {{date}} #> 2 {{domain}}   TRTSDT ady$outputs #> [1] "{{variable}}"

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: Rendering fills Mustache placeholders concrete values. $render() method takes parameters named arguments returns mighty_component_rendered object. Notice every {{ }} placeholder now concrete name: convenience function combines retrieval rendering one step. returns rendered component . Note get_rendered_component() takes parameters named list, unlike $render() takes ...: omit required parameter, get informative error:
code: ady_rendered <- ady$render(domain = "adae", variable = "ASTDY", date = "ASTDT") ady_rendered #> <mighty_component_rendered/mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: derivation #> Depends: #> • adae.ASTDT #> • adae.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • ASTDY #> Code: #> adae <- adae |> #>   dplyr::mutate( #>     ASTDY = admiral::compute_duration( #>       start_date = TRTSDT, #>       end_date = ASTDT, #>       in_unit = "days", #>       out_unit = "days", #>       add_one = TRUE #>     ) #>   ) get_rendered_component(   "ady",   list(domain = "adae", variable = "ASTDY", date = "ASTDT") ) #> <mighty_component_rendered/mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: derivation #> Depends: #> • adae.ASTDT #> • adae.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • ASTDY #> Code: #> adae <- adae |> #>   dplyr::mutate( #>     ASTDY = admiral::compute_duration( #>       start_date = TRTSDT, #>       end_date = ASTDT, #>       in_unit = "days", #>       out_unit = "days", #>       add_one = TRUE #>     ) #>   ) ady$render(domain = "adae") #> Error in `ms_render()`: #> ! Parameter names not matching component requirements: #> ✖ `variable` not specified #> ✖ `date` not specified

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: rendered, call $eval() execute code current environment. component code contains assignment (e.g., adae <- adae |> ...), $eval() evaluates code calling environment via eval(envir = parent.frame()). means $eval() modifies domain variable place — assignment return value needed. ASTDY column exist yet. Run rendered component: $eval() executes rendered code calling environment default. can pass different environment via envir argument needed. want save rendered code script file instead evaluating interactively, use $stream(path) append code R file.
code: adae <- pharmaverseadam::adae |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, ASTDT, TRTSDT)  names(adae) #> [1] "USUBJID" "ASTDT"   "TRTSDT" ady_rendered$eval() #> → Evaluating component Analysis relative day #> ℹ Code: #> `adae <- adae |>`, ` dplyr::mutate(`, ` ASTDY = admiral::compute_duration(`, ` #> start_date = TRTSDT,`, ` end_date = ASTDT,`, ` in_unit = "days",`, ` out_unit = #> "days",`, ` add_one = TRUE`, ` )`, and ` )` names(adae) #> [1] "USUBJID" "ASTDT"   "TRTSDT"  "ASTDY" head(adae) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   USUBJID     ASTDT      TRTSDT     ASTDY #>   <chr>       <date>     <date>     <dbl> #> 1 01-701-1015 2014-01-03 2014-01-02     2 #> 2 01-701-1015 2014-01-03 2014-01-02     2 #> 3 01-701-1015 2014-01-09 2014-01-02     8 #> 4 01-701-1023 2012-08-07 2012-08-05     3 #> 5 01-701-1023 2012-08-07 2012-08-05     3 #> 6 01-701-1023 2012-08-07 2012-08-05     3

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: can author components .mustache files. realistic example derives ratio current value baseline (R2BASE) lab parameter. Save following template .mustache file: saving template .mustache file, load, render, run :
code: #' @title Ratio to baseline #' @description #' Derives the ratio of the analysis value to the baseline value. #' #' @param domain `character` Name of the domain #' @param variable `character` Name of the new ratio variable #' @type derivation #' @depends {{domain}} AVAL #' @depends {{domain}} BASE #' @outputs {{variable}} #' @code {{domain}} <- {{domain}} |>   dplyr::mutate(     {{variable}} = dplyr::if_else(BASE != 0, AVAL / BASE, NA_real_)   ) r2base <- get_component(r2base_file) r2base #> <mighty_component/R6> #> /tmp/RtmpmInIzX/file1ad755841ddf.mustache: Derives the ratio of the analysis #> value to the baseline value. #> Type: derivation #> Parameters: #> • domain: `character` Name of the domain #> • variable: `character` Name of the new ratio variable #> Depends: #> • {{domain}}.AVAL #> • {{domain}}.BASE #> Outputs: #> • {{variable}} r2base_rendered <- r2base$render(   domain = "adlb",   variable = "R2BASE" ) r2base_rendered$code #> [1] "adlb <- adlb |>"                                               #> [2] "  dplyr::mutate("                                              #> [3] "    R2BASE = dplyr::if_else(BASE != 0, AVAL / BASE, NA_real_)" #> [4] "  )" adlb <- pharmaverseadam::adlb |>   dplyr::filter(PARAMCD == "ALB") |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVISIT, AVAL, BASE)  head(adlb) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   USUBJID     PARAMCD AVISIT                 AVAL  BASE #>   <chr>       <chr>   <chr>                 <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 01-701-1015 ALB     Baseline                 38    38 #> 2 01-701-1015 ALB     Week 2                   39    38 #> 3 01-701-1015 ALB     POST-BASELINE MAXIMUM    39    38 #> 4 01-701-1015 ALB     Week 4                   38    38 #> 5 01-701-1015 ALB     Week 6                   37    38 #> 6 01-701-1015 ALB     POST-BASELINE MINIMUM    37    38  r2base_rendered$eval() #> → Evaluating component Ratio to baseline #> ℹ Code: #> `adlb <- adlb |>`, ` dplyr::mutate(`, ` R2BASE = dplyr::if_else(BASE != 0, AVAL #> / BASE, NA_real_)`, and ` )`  adlb |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVISIT, AVAL, BASE, R2BASE) |>   head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   USUBJID     PARAMCD AVISIT                 AVAL  BASE R2BASE #>   <chr>       <chr>   <chr>                 <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 01-701-1015 ALB     Baseline                 38    38  1     #> 2 01-701-1015 ALB     Week 2                   39    38  1.03  #> 3 01-701-1015 ALB     POST-BASELINE MAXIMUM    39    38  1.03  #> 4 01-701-1015 ALB     Week 4                   38    38  1     #> 5 01-701-1015 ALB     Week 6                   37    38  0.974 #> 6 01-701-1015 ALB     POST-BASELINE MINIMUM    37    38  0.974

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: component rendered, generated code automatically validated. package currently checks implicit joins — dplyr::left_join(), dplyr::inner_join(), similar call without explicit argument triggers error. prevents common source bugs clinical programming join columns change studies. component fails validation: fix specify join key explicitly:
code: #' @title Bad join example #' @description Implicit join that will fail validation. #' #' @param domain `character` domain name #' @type predecessor #' @depends {{domain}} USUBJID #' @outputs NEWCOL #' @code {{domain}} <- {{domain}} |>   dplyr::left_join(other_data) get_rendered_component(bad_file, list(domain = "adae")) #> Error in `abort_validation_errors()`: #> ! Component validation failed: #>  #> ! Implicit dplyr join(s) detected in rendered component: #> ✖ Join operations must explicitly specify the `by` argument #> ℹ Line 2: dplyr::left_join #' @title Good join example #' @description Explicit join that passes validation. #' #' @param domain `character` domain name #' @type predecessor #' @depends {{domain}} USUBJID #' @outputs NEWCOL #' @code {{domain}} <- {{domain}} |>   dplyr::left_join(other_data, by = dplyr::join_by(USUBJID)) get_rendered_component(good_file, list(domain = "adae"))$code #> [1] "adae <- adae |>"                                              #> [2] "  dplyr::left_join(other_data, by = dplyr::join_by(USUBJID))"

title: Getting Started with mighty.component
text: get_test_component() creates component runs isolated R session automatic code coverage tracking. useful interactive exploration formal unit tests testthat. set check_coverage = FALSE code runs inside vignette, inside test_that() block. default (TRUE) uses withr::defer() automatically verify coverage test finishes — use default actual tests. Assign input data isolated session: Execute component retrieve result: Check coverage — every line component code executed: check_coverage = TRUE (default), coverage verified automatically test object goes scope using withr::defer(). line executed, error raised. integrates naturally testthat test files: create test component inside test_that() block, assign data, evaluate, assert results — coverage checking happens automatically test finishes.
code: ady_test <- get_test_component(   component = "ady",   params = list(domain = "adae", variable = "ASTDY", date = "ASTDT"),   check_coverage = FALSE ) ady_test #> <mighty_component_test/mighty_component_rendered/mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Test Coverage: 0.00% #> Code: (✔ Covered, ✖ Uncovered) #> ✖ adae <- adae |> #> ✖   dplyr::mutate( #> ✖     ASTDY = admiral::compute_duration( #> ✖       start_date = TRTSDT, #> ✖       end_date = ASTDT, #> ✖       in_unit = "days", #> ✖       out_unit = "days", #> ✖       add_one = TRUE #> ✖     ) #> ✖   ) adae_input <- pharmaverseadam::adae |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, ASTDT, TRTSDT)  ady_test$assign("adae", adae_input) ady_test$ls() #> [1] "adae" ady_test$eval() ady_test$get("adae") |> head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   USUBJID     ASTDT      TRTSDT     ASTDY #>   <chr>       <date>     <date>     <dbl> #> 1 01-701-1015 2014-01-03 2014-01-02     2 #> 2 01-701-1015 2014-01-03 2014-01-02     2 #> 3 01-701-1015 2014-01-09 2014-01-02     8 #> 4 01-701-1023 2012-08-07 2012-08-05     3 #> 5 01-701-1023 2012-08-07 2012-08-05     3 #> 6 01-701-1023 2012-08-07 2012-08-05     3 # Normal print method ady_test #> <mighty_component_test/mighty_component_rendered/mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Test Coverage: 100.00% #> Code: (✔ Covered, ✖ Uncovered) #> ✔ adae <- adae |> #> ✔   dplyr::mutate( #> ✔     ASTDY = admiral::compute_duration( #> ✔       start_date = TRTSDT, #> ✔       end_date = ASTDT, #> ✔       in_unit = "days", #> ✔       out_unit = "days", #> ✔       add_one = TRUE #> ✔     ) #> ✔   ) # Percent coverage ady_test$percent_coverage #> [1] 100 # Line coverage in a data.frame ady_test$line_coverage #>    line value #> 1     1     1 #> 2     2     1 #> 3     3     1 #> 4     4     1 #> 5     5     1 #> 6     6     1 #> 7     7     1 #> 8     8     1 #> 9     9     1 #> 10   10     1

title: Mighty Components for ADaM Generation
text: mighty.component serve repository generic compute component classes used produce ADaM scripts {mighty} framework.

title: Mighty Components for ADaM Generation
text: can install development version mighty.component GitHub :

title: Mighty Components for ADaM Generation
text: mighty.component provide generic classes work mighty components, helper functions retrieve . now also holds small temporary library components. See vignette("mighty-component") work components.

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
text: Retrieve mighty code component, supporting built-standards custom components local files. get_component(): Returns object class mighty_component containing standard custom component. get_rendered_component(): Returns object class mighty_component_rendered containing rendered code component rendering component required list parameters depends individual component. Check documentation specific standard, local component, details.

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
text: component character specifying either standard component name path custom component file (R Mustache template). params named list input parameters. Passed along mighty_component$render().

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
text: Processes different component types based file extension component name: extension: Looks built-standard components name. .R: Extracts renders custom functions. .mustache: Creates components template files.

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
code: get_component("ady") #> <mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: derivation #> Parameters: #> • domain: `character` Name of new domain being created #> • variable: `character` Name of new variable to create #> • date: `character` Name of date variable to use #> Depends: #> • {{domain}}.{{date}} #> • {{domain}}.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • {{variable}}  get_rendered_component("ady", list(domain = "advs", variable = "ASTDY", date = "ASTDT")) #> <mighty_component_rendered/mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: derivation #> Depends: #> • advs.ASTDT #> • advs.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • ASTDY #> Code: #> advs <- advs |> #>   dplyr::mutate( #>     ASTDY = admiral::compute_duration( #>       start_date = TRTSDT, #>       end_date = ASTDT, #>       in_unit = "days", #>       out_unit = "days", #>       add_one = TRUE #>     ) #>   )

title: Retrieve mighty standard component — get_standard
text: Retrieve either generalized standard component (template) rendered standard component code ready use. get_standard(): Returns object class mighty_component get_rendered_standard(): Returns object class mighty_component_rendered rendering standard required list parameters depends standard. Check documentation specific standard details.

title: Retrieve mighty standard component — get_standard
text: standard character name standard component retrieve. params named list input parameters. Passed along mighty_component$render().

title: Retrieve mighty standard component — get_standard
code: get_standard("ady") #> <mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: derivation #> Parameters: #> • domain: `character` Name of new domain being created #> • variable: `character` Name of new variable to create #> • date: `character` Name of date variable to use #> Depends: #> • {{domain}}.{{date}} #> • {{domain}}.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • {{variable}}  get_rendered_standard("ady", list(domain = "advs", variable = "ASTDY", date = "ASTDT")) #> <mighty_component_rendered/mighty_component/R6> #> ady: Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: derivation #> Depends: #> • advs.ASTDT #> • advs.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • ASTDY #> Code: #> advs <- advs |> #>   dplyr::mutate( #>     ASTDY = admiral::compute_duration( #>       start_date = TRTSDT, #>       end_date = ASTDT, #>       in_unit = "days", #>       out_unit = "days", #>       add_one = TRUE #>     ) #>   )

what: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
text: Creates mighty_component_test object rendered component, enabling structured unit testing optional coverage checking. See mighty_component_test description testing workflow.

title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
code: get_test_component(   component,   params = list(),   check_coverage = TRUE,   teardown_env = parent.frame() )

title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
text: component character specifying either standard component name path custom component file (R Mustache template). params named list input parameters. Passed along mighty_component$render(). check_coverage logical(1) Whether automatically check test coverage test completes. TRUE (default), coverage verified via test_component$check_coverage() deferred call. teardown_env environment register deferred coverage check. Defaults caller's environment (parent.frame()). controls check_coverage() executes test teardown.

title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
text: mighty_component_test object.

title: List all available standards — list_standards
code: # Simple character list of all standard ids: list_standards() #> [1] "ady"         "aendt"       "assign"      "astdt"       "predecessor" #> [6] "supp_sdtm"   "trtemfl"      # Tibble for an easy overview list_standards(as = "tibble") #> # A tibble: 7 × 7 #>   id          title                     description params depends outputs code  #>   <chr>       <chr>                     <chr>       <list> <list>  <list>  <lis> #> 1 ady         Analysis relative day     Derives th… <df>   <df>    <chr>   <chr> #> 2 aendt       Analysis end date         Derives an… <df>   <df>    <chr>   <chr> #> 3 assign      Assign                    Assigns a … <df>   <NULL>  <chr>   <chr> #> 4 astdt       Analysis start date       Derives an… <df>   <df>    <chr>   <chr> #> 5 predecessor Predecessor               Creates ne… <df>   <df>    <chr>   <chr> #> 6 supp_sdtm   Add Supplementary Variab… Add a vari… <df>   <df>    <chr>   <chr> #> 7 trtemfl     Treatment-emergent flag   Derives tr… <df>   <df>    <chr>   <chr>  # List (only showing first 2): list_standards(as = "list") |>   head(2) |>   str() #> List of 2 #>  $ :List of 7 #>   ..$ id         : chr "ady" #>   ..$ title      : chr "Analysis relative day" #>   ..$ description: chr "Derives the relative day compared to the treatment start date." #>   ..$ params     :'data.frame':	3 obs. of  2 variables: #>   .. ..$ name       : chr [1:3] "domain" "variable" "date" #>   .. ..$ description: chr [1:3] "`character` Name of new domain being created" "`character` Name of new variable to create" "`character` Name of date variable to use" #>   ..$ depends    :'data.frame':	2 obs. of  2 variables: #>   .. ..$ domain: chr [1:2] "{{domain}}" "{{domain}}" #>   .. ..$ column: chr [1:2] "{{date}}" "TRTSDT" #>   ..$ outputs    : chr "{{variable}}" #>   ..$ code       : chr [1:10] "{{domain}} <- {{domain}} |>" "  dplyr::mutate(" "    {{variable}} = admiral::compute_duration(" "      start_date = TRTSDT," ... #>  $ :List of 7 #>   ..$ id         : chr "aendt" #>   ..$ title      : chr "Analysis end date" #>   ..$ description: chr "Derives analysis end date based on (incomplete) dates given as character" #>   ..$ params     :'data.frame':	2 obs. of  2 variables: #>   .. ..$ name       : chr [1:2] "domain" "dtc" #>   .. ..$ description: chr [1:2] "`character` Name of new domain being created" "`character` Name of date variable" #>   ..$ depends    :'data.frame':	1 obs. of  2 variables: #>   .. ..$ domain: chr "{{domain}}" #>   .. ..$ column: chr "{{dtc}}" #>   ..$ outputs    : chr [1:2] "AENDT" "AENDTF" #>   ..$ code       : chr [1:12] "{{domain}} <- {{domain}} |>" "  dplyr::mutate(" "    AENDT = admiral::convert_dtc_to_dt(" "      dtc = {{dtc}}," ...

title: Options for mighty.component — mighty.component-options
text: Verbosity level functions mighty.component. See zephyr::verbosity_level details. Default: NA_character_ Option: mighty.component.verbosity_level Environment: R_MIGHTY.COMPONENT_VERBOSITY_LEVEL

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: Class generic mighty component. mighty framework, "component" code template processes input data returns modified version new columns rows. Mighty components share common structure roxygen-like documentation pattern, facilitating use inside mighty.

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: Templates character vectors R code interpreted. Dynamic use variables etc. supported using mustache framework. Dynamic parameters specified using {{ variable_name }}.

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: template required documented following tags similar documenting functions using roxygen2:

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: template standard components follow conventions: input data set always called {{ domain }}. Additional parameters used render template R code documented @param tag. template ends creating modified version {{ domain }}. Template documented roxygen-like tags

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: example mighty component template creates new dynamic variable variable twice value dynamic input x, already input data set {{ domain }}.   rendered parameters variable = "" x = "B" rendered code used mighty becomes:
code: #' @title Title for my component #' @description #' A more in depth description of what is being done #' #' @param variable dynamic output if applicable #' @param x some other input to the component #' @type derivation #' @depends {{ domain }} {{ x }} #' @outputs {{ variable }} #' @code {{ domain }} <- {{ domain }} |>   dplyr::mutate(     {{ variable }} = 2 * {{ x }}   ) {{ domain }} <- {{ domain }} |>   dplyr::mutate(     A = 2 * B   )

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: id Component ID. title Title component. description Description component. code code block component. template complete template. type type component. Can one predecessor, derivation, row. depends Data.frame listing components dependencies. outputs List new columns created component. params Data.frame listing parameters need supplied rendering component.

title: Test mighty component — mighty_component_test
text: Class unit testing mighty component code coverage tracking. Runs component code isolated R session tracks lines executed testing.

title: Test mighty component — mighty_component_test
text: Always use get_test_component() create instances testing. test workflow : Create test component get_test_component() Assign input data $assign() Execute track coverage $eval() Retrieve results $get() Test results expect_*() functions {testthat} Coverage automatically checked test teardown via $check_coverage().
